title: Domains registry (per-domain authoring contract)
description: Per-domain authoring contract for the knowledge substrate: where each domain's source lives, which schema governs its frontmatter, how its markdown body is handled (the orthogonal bodyMode axes), and its dist shape. Hand-authored source of truth; STEP 4's unified derive engine will read it.

_note.description: Free-text provenance note for human readers (not consumed by tooling).
examples: Per-domain authoring contract. Hand-authored source of truth.

domains.description: Map of unit key → authoring-contract unit. Keys are domain names; domains that split by record-type use `domain/recordType` (e.g. `app-context/apps`).

summary.description: One-line human description of the domain and how its body is handled.
examples: Component category defaults — body mirrored verbatim to dist .md.

src.description: Source location glob (or exact file); null for body-less domains derived from dist (e.g. graph).

generator.description: The derive script that produces this domain's dist; null for hand-authored data files with no derive.

frontmatterSchema.description: JSON Schema governing this domain's frontmatter; null when unschematized (a gap to close in a later slice).

body.description: Orthogonal body-handling axes: whether the markdown body is structurally parsed, copied verbatim, and/or normalized into a single named field.

mirror.description: True if the body is also copied verbatim into dist (a .md mirror or a verbatim field).

field.description: Name of the single field the body is normalized into (field-extraction), or null.

wysiwyg.description: Per-file dist-equivalence config for domains whose Milkdown body round-trip is proven safe per file (foundations, accessibility, guidelines). The rich-safe file SET is no longer an allowlist here: it is CI-generated by walking + round-tripping every editable file (editor/src/generated/wysiwyg-safe-paths.json, produced by editor/scripts/gen-wysiwyg-safe-paths.ts). Domains proven dist-safe holistically by a CI derive-and-diff gate (content/*) declare no wysiwyg block.

distEquivalence.description: Per-file dist-equivalence config. Tells the drift guard which engine to compute the structured view it compares across a Milkdown round-trip, and how to call it. Absent for domains whose dist-safety is proven holistically by a CI derive-and-diff gate (e.g. content/global, content/patterns).

engine.description: The drift-guard equivalence engine. 'section-dist' (scripts/lib/section-dist/index.js) parses the body into a Pattern-H file tree — used by foundations/accessibility, requires sourceRel. 'guideline-sections' (scripts/components/guideline-md-parser.js) parses the body into the per-component sections[] array, accepting the verbatim markdown-field drift as rendered-equivalent — used by the guidelines domain, needs no further options.

sourceRel.description: Repo-relative source prefix passed as the sourceRel option to deriveFromMarkdown (e.g. 'foundations/src/'). Required by the section-dist engine; unused by guideline-sections.

rootAnchor.description: Optional rootAnchor option passed to deriveFromMarkdown. Required by domains whose top-level anchor differs from the default.

applySkipH2Slugs.description: When true the guard imports SKIP_H2_SLUGS from derive-foundations.js and passes it as skipH2Slugs. Only foundations currently needs this.
